sections.title: 1. PROSE: Earth, the Desolated Home
sections.content:
Story Overview

  • 🚀 Setting: The year 2068. Earth is completely destroyed due to human exploitation.
  • 🛰️ New Habitat: Humans have colonized Mars (the Red Planet). India has established three colonies there.
  • 👨‍👩‍👦 Characters: Arivumathi and her grandfather.
  • 📖 Plot Synopsis: Grandfather travels 1500 sols (Martian days) to visit Arivumathi for her birthday. He compares the harsh, artificial life on Mars with the beautiful, natural past of Earth.
  • 🌱 Core Message: We must protect our natural home (Earth) instead of destroying it and struggling to adapt to dead planets.

Key Comparisons (Earth vs. Mars)

Feature Life on Earth (Past) Life on Mars (Year 2068)
Environment Clean, natural, and free air. Toxic; requires a space suit and oxygen cylinder.
Agriculture Natural, fertile soil. Vegetables were tasty. Soil is chemically treated. Crops lack real flavor.
Water Supply Naturally available from rain and rivers. Artificially generated by burning fuels.

sections.title: 2. POEM: Beyond the Universe
sections.content:
Theme: A vivid, imaginative journey across the solar system, exploring the unique characteristics of different planets.

The Cosmic Journey:

  • 🌍 1. Earth: Leaving behind our blue home.
  • 🔴 2. Mars: Gliding past the red planet.
  • 🪐 3. Saturn: Marveling at its magnificent rings and moons.
  • 🌀 4. Jupiter: Observing the solar system's giant, characterized by its red storms.
  • 🔃 5. Uranus: Passing the tumbling, rolling planet.
  • ❄️ 6. Neptune: Flying past the blue ice giant.
  • 🌌 7. Final Destination: Reaching beyond the Milky Way galaxy, soaring into the infinite universe to meet the Creator.

sections.title: 3. SUPPLEMENTARY: Lost in Space
sections.content:
Story Overview

  • Main Characters: Two friends, Amuthan and Nilavan.
  • The Incident: While visiting a space museum, they accidentally lock themselves inside a functional space shuttle. They accidentally pull a lever, launching themselves into outer space.
  • The Climax: The shuttle crash-lands on a strange, unknown planet. They are terrified until friendly green aliens approach them.
  • Resolution: The aliens peacefully repair the boys' shuttle, open the locked doors, and safely guide them back to Earth.

sections.title: 4. GLOSSARY & PICTIONARY
sections.content:
Vocabulary Word Definition / Meaning
DesolatedMade empty, uninhabitable, ruined, or bare.
HubA habitat; a central living space or settlement.
SolA Martian day (a day on planet Mars).
CapsuleA small cylinder-shaped bed or compartment for sleeping.
Space suitA specialized, pressurized suit worn by astronauts.
AstonishedGreatly surprised, amazed, or impressed.
Fossil FuelFuel formed from the remains of dead plants and animals over millions of years (e.g., coal, oil).
AdaptedModified or adjusted to survive in new conditions.
GalaxyA massive system consisting of millions or billions of stars.
Milky WayThe specific galaxy that contains our Solar System.

sections.title: 5. GRAMMAR (Let us Build & Let us Know)
sections.content:
A. Compound Words

A compound word is formed when two distinct words are combined to create a brand-new word with a completely different meaning.

  • ⭐ Sun + Flower = Sunflower
  • ⭐ Light + House = Lighthouse
  • ⭐ Mail + Box = Mailbox
  • ⭐ Watch + Man = Watchman
  • ⭐ Star + Fish = Starfish
  • ⭐ Rain + Bow = Rainbow
  • ⭐ Foot + Ball = Football

B. Nouns

1. Collective Noun: Names a group viewed as a single, whole unit.

  • 🐦 A flock of birds
  • 🐝 A swarm of bees
  • 🍇 A bunch of grapes
  • 🐜 An army of ants
  • 🐄 A herd of cattle
  • 🎵 A choir of singers

2. Abstract Noun: Names concepts, emotions, or qualities that cannot be physically touched.

  • 💭 Emotions: Happiness, sadness, anger, fear
  • 💡 Ideas: Freedom, justice, knowledge, truth
  • ✨ Qualities: Honesty, bravery, beauty, kindness

qa.q: What is the setting of the story 'Earth, the Desolated Home'?
qa.a: The story is set in the future, in the year 2068, on the planet Mars. Earth has been rendered uninhabitable due to human actions.

qa.q: How long did it take for the grandfather to reach Arivumathi?
qa.a: It took the grandfather 1500 sols (Martian days) to travel across Mars to reach Arivumathi's hub.

qa.q: How is water produced on Mars according to the lesson?
qa.a: Unlike Earth, where water is naturally available, water on Mars is artificially produced by burning fuels.

qa.q: Name the planets mentioned in the poem 'Beyond the Universe'.
qa.a: The poet mentions Earth, Mars, Saturn, Jupiter, Uranus, and Neptune during the space journey.

qa.q: How did Amuthan and Nilavan return to Earth?
qa.a: After crashing on an unknown planet, friendly green aliens approached them, repaired their damaged space shuttle, and safely guided them back home.
